can not only accelerate the training but also
accelerate inference, which is a promising direction in the future.
In this part, the whole workflow
definitions used in quantization recipe documents,
scaling method and KV cache & SDPA quantization flow. They are device agnostic and defined in this document

In general, the output data type of low-
K GEMM): The output generated by this operation generally utilizes FP32 to maintain high numerical accuracy as it is passed into the softmax function.

V GEMM): The output produced by this operation feeds into the output projection layer and can be reduced to a lower-bit format, accelerating the subsequent projection GEMM through low-precision computation

Please refer to the appendix for the details of each dimension

Low precision tuning becomes more popular in the pretraining and finetuning scenarios, such as DeepSeek models that pretrained with FP8, gpt-oss model that finetuned with MXFP4. In this recipe, we will explore some low precision recipes on training, including BF
, or some mixed/hybrid precision recipes, like MXFP4 forward with MXFP8 backward, etc.
For the training frameworks, we utilize Megatron-LM, which is more efficient for training
For the models, we support the
For the datasets, we select some reasoning datasets to improve the LLMs reasoning performance to conduct low precision finetuning experiments. We select 2 reasoning datasets:
For the benchmarks, non-reasoning benchmarks, such as mmlu, gsm8k, piqa, hellaswag and reasoning benchmarks, such as gpqa_diamod, math500, gsm8k (reasoning).
the common recipe for BF16 finetuning and the additional recipe for low-precision fine-tuning.
global batch size=8, micro batch size=2
BF16. The error is less than 3%
since the magnitude of the loss is too small.
The reasoning benchmarks show that the accuracy of
The general benchmarks show that the accuracy of
the common recipe for BF16 finetuning and the additional recipe for low-precision fine-tuning.
global batch size=8, micro batch size=
BF16. The error is less than
mxfp8 > fp8 > b16, is that we
mxfp8/fp8 needs more gpu memory, which results in a low training speed.
The reasoning benchmarks show that the accuracy of FP8/MXFP8 loss < 1% compared to BF16 after SFT.
The general benchmarks show that the accuracy of FP8/MXFP8 loss < 1% compared to BF16 after SFT.
